headline: After Losing Both Legs, 28-Year-Old Finds New Purpose
meta_description: A young man who lost both legs in a road accident five years ago now delivers meals on a modified motorcycle, refusing to let his injury define him.
summary_short: After losing both legs in a road accident, 28-year-old Hamza Ali now delivers meals riding a specially modified motorcycle fitted with hand controls.
body:
Five years after a road accident took both his legs, 28-year-old Hamza Ali refuses to be defined by his injury. Today, he delivers meals riding a modified motorcycle equipped with hand-operated controls—a testament to his determination to build an independent life.

Hamza's choice to work as a meal delivery driver shows both practical problem-solving and emotional resilience. Rather than accepting a passive role, he adapted the tools available to him and carved out meaningful work. His motorcycle, fitted with hand controls, allows him to maintain mobility and earn a living on his own terms.

His story challenges assumptions about what's possible after life-altering injury. By turning his circumstances into a platform for action, Hamza demonstrates that limitation and purpose aren't mutually exclusive—and that recovery isn't always about returning to the past, but about building something new.
quick_facts: Hamza Ali lost both legs in a road accident five years ago
Now 28 years old, he delivers meals for a living
He rides a modified motorcycle with hand-operated controls
He refused to let his injury define his future
